round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent successfully integrated cross-agent evidence (regulatory curb standards, meta utility poles) to strengthen the Yucatán hypothesis from medium to high confidence.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent effectively synthesized landscape (soil) and regulatory (curbs) evidence to refine its botanical assessment and increase confidence.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent incorporated landscape and meta observations to reinforce the diagnostic value of the yellow curbs, moving from medium to high confidence.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent synthesized landscape (soil) and botanics (forest) evidence to confirm the regional profile, increasing confidence to high.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the strong consensus from the landscape, regulatory, and meta agents regarding the Yucatán Peninsula (pale soil, yellow curbs, concrete poles). The linguistic agent's lack of evidence was correctly noted. The final decision to select Mexico was well-supported by the convergence of infrastructure and environmental cues.
